mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, with Rank IC > 0.05 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 55% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE jump.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of observations, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 50% of cases during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings above 3.5% annually force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields negative 20-day forward returns in > 55% of observations, or interaction Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases aggregate corporate earnings potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical relationship with 60-day SPX returns, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02 across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production decline exceeding 0.5% signals abrupt supply-side contraction, triggering earnings downgrades and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following a >0.5% INDPRO drop.
